index 0450e46..28f8469 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -319,2 +319,21 @@
   - No new R&D this week; preserve cash while existing tier 4/5/6 projects mature.
 - Watch next week: cash recovery after compute rollback, S2 conversion with A3 post-R&D quality, churn after the $119/tier4 week, issue age, and whether S2 cap starts to sharply limit leads.
+
+## Week 20 / Day 133
+
+- Cash: $2,127,029. Subscribers: 136,054. Week 19 added only 6,947 subscribers from 16,422 leads; S2 saturation is now clear. Cancellations rose to 2,829.
+- Tier 4 R&D completed, raising global bonus to 0.4344. S2 A quality on tier 3 is now 1.6286, so tier 4 model spend is unnecessary for S2.
+- Week 19 cash flow: subscription payments $3,065,760; compute -$947,326; ops -$745,500; ads -$560,000; capacity -$196,000; dev -$30,100; lead acquisition -$8,820. Cash recovered after rolling A/B back to tier 3.
+- S2 paid channel is near marginal: content produced 12,608 A leads and 6,075 subscribers at $80k/day spend. It is still positive but no longer worth aggressive scaling.
+- Enterprise now looks viable for D_E08/D_E02/D_E09, and E3 may be viable through a higher-quality C plan. E2 remains too quality-demanding.
+- Changes:
+  - Started R&D tier 7 for $1,166,667. Bad sample: expected completion day 211, expected boost only +0.001. Treat as sunk lottery ticket.
+  - Started group research to level 2 for D_E08, D_E02, D_E09, and E3, expected day 136.
+  - Cut S2 content ads from $80k/day to $40k/day.
+  - Started enterprise LinkedIn tests: D_E08 $20k/day, D_E02 $15k/day, D_E09 $10k/day, E3 $10k/day.
+  - Set model tiers A3/B3/C5. C5 is to support enterprise quality without increasing individual A compute.
+  - Kept prices A/B/C at $109/$279/$499.
+  - Ops set to $119k/day total: global $25k, S2 $75k, S3 $1k, D_E08 $5k, D_E02 $5k, D_E09 $3k, E3 $5k.
+  - Dev set to $19.2k/day total, focused on enterprise target groups plus S2 maintenance.
+  - Capacity remains tier 6.
+- Watch next week: enterprise lead inbox, research level 2 results, cash after tier 7 spend, S2 churn, and whether reduced S2 ads stabilize CAC.
